data.title: Freddie Mac says no more work-from-home by May - report
data.description:
Ending its remote work flexibility, Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) told staffers to return to the office five days a week starting in May, with a promise to review pay plans to reward key employees, Bloomberg reported on Thursday, citing a Wednesday memo from Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Bill Pulte and Freddie CEO Diana Reid.

As of Jan. 31, the mortgage giant employed more than 8,000 workers.

Employees have about 45 days to come back to the office, ensuring its critical function in the U.S. mortgage market remains uninterrupted, a person familiar with the matter told Bloomberg.

The move comes as Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) and Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) are banking on President Donald Trump's administration to dismantle the federal conservatorship that's controlled both entities since the 2008 foreclosure bust.

FMCC shares gained 0.4% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: Golar LNG signs finance lease agreements for FLNG Gimi
data.description:
• Golar LNG (NASDAQ:GLNG) has entered into finance lease agreements with a consortium of leading Chinese leasing companies for the refinancing of the existing FLNG Gimi debt facility, it said on Thursday.
  • The sale leaseback facility will be approximately $1.2 billion.
  • The contemplated sale and leaseback facility will have a tenor of 12 years and a 17-year amortization profile, with quarterly repayment installments throughout the lease period. 
  • Upon closing and repayment of the existing debt facility, Gimi MS is expected to generate net proceeds of about $530 million.
  • Golar LNG (NASDAQ:GLNG) will benefit from 70% of these proceeds, equivalent to approximately $371 million.

data.title: JOYY declares $0.93 dividend
data.description:
• JOYY (NASDAQ:YY) declares $0.93/ADS quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 7.94%
  • Payable April 30; for shareholders of record April 17; ex-div April 17.
  • See YY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Diana Shipping enters time charter contract for m/v DSI Andromeda with Cargill
data.description:
• Diana Shipping (NYSE:DSX) through a separate wholly-owned subsidiary, has entered into a time charter  contract with Cargill Ocean Transportation (Singapore) Pte, for one of its Ultramax dry bulk vessels, the m/v DSI Andromeda.
  • The gross charter rate is $14,000 per day, minus a 4.75% commission paid to third parties, for a period until minimum November 15, 2025 up to maximum January 15, 2026.
  • The charter is expected to commence on March 29, 2025.
  • The m/v DSI Andromeda is currently chartered to Bunge SA, Geneva, at a gross charter rate of $13,500 per day, minus a 5.00% commission paid to third parties.
  • The employment of “DSI Andromeda” is anticipated to generate approximately $3.18 million of gross revenue for the minimum scheduled period of the time charter.

data.title: Jabil jumps after increasing outlook; CEO highlights advantage of domestic manufacturing
data.description:
Jabil's (NYSE:JBL) second quarter fiscal 2025 financial results easily topped consensus estimates, and the company also increased its 2025 outlook, prompting shares to jump during early trading on Thursday.

The contract manufacturer of electrical components now expects revenue of $27.9B for the entirety of fiscal 2025 versus prior guidance of $27.3B. It now projects earnings per share of $8.95, which is more than its earlier guidance of $8.75 and the consensus estimate of $8.74.

Jabil's third quarter revenue forecast ranges from $6.7B to $7.3B, with a midpoint of $7B, which is well above the $6.77B estimate. Earnings per share for the quarter are expected to range from $2.08 to $2.48, with a midpoint of $2.28 more than the $2.22 consensus.

For the second quarter of fiscal 2025, which ended Feb. 28, 2025, Jabil reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.94, which was more than the estimate of $1.83. Revenue totaled $6.73B, which was also more than the $6.4B consensus.

Jabil CEO Mike Dastoor highlighted his company's advantage of having a significant U.S. manufacturing footprint.

"In Q2, we exceeded our expectations due to continued strength in our capital equipment, cloud and data center infrastructure, and digital commerce end-markets," Dastoor said. "As the geopolitical situation continues to evolve, our ability to adapt, combined with our designation as a U.S. domiciled manufacturing service provider and our significant U.S. footprint, is becoming increasingly important for our customers." 

Jabil operates in 30 countries, but has a significant number of manufacturing and engineering locations in the U.S.

data.title: Oklo announces milestones on path to deploy first commercial powerhouse in Idaho
data.description:
Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) said Thursday it reached a milestone agreement with Idaho National Laboratory, the U.S. Department of Energy's largest multi-program science and technology research lab, as it prepares to lay the foundations of its first commercial nuclear power plant.

The agreement, reached in collaboration with the DoE and local Shoshone Bannock Tribes, aims to ensure that Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) follows environmental regulations throughout the site investigation process for the future location of its Aurora nuclear powerhouse.

Oklo (OKLO) has been focusing on its timeline to get nuclear reactors on the ground, targeting first deployment by 2027, and has signed deals with partners including data center operator Switch and fuel developer Lightbridge.

In a Barron's interview, Oklo (OKLO) CEO Jacob DeWitte said the latest development was both a sign of how far the company had come and of how far it still has to go.

The latest agreement is "the byproduct of our broader partnerships that have been progressing with the Department of Energy and Idaho National Laboratory to actually get this thing up and running," the CEO said. "So it's a significant milestone, but there's more to come."

data.title: Solventum reaffirms 2025 guidance, and announces long-range plan through 2028
data.description:
• Solventum (NYSE:SOLV) on Thursday reaffirmed its fiscal year 2025 guidance, originally provided in connection with its Q4 earnings, and announced its LRP for 2025 through 2028.
  • The company expects to accelerate organic sales growth of 4% to 5% in 2028, expand operating margins to be 23% to 25% in 2028, generate EPS CAGR of 10% over the 3-year period and drive free cash flow conversion of 80%+.
  • The company is also focused on delivering attractive ROIC and a solid investment grade credit rating, and intends to use the proceeds from the P&F transaction primarily to pay down debt.
  • Solventum said it will consider share repurchases and dividends over time.
  • Solventum (SOLV) shares were up 1.68% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Apple TV+ nears 45M subscribers, losing $1B annually: report
data.description:
• Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) streaming service, Apple TV+, has approached 45M subscribers, though it is still losing around $1B annually, The Information reported.
  • The company has spent around $5B a year on content since 2019, though that number has since been trimmed to around $4.5B, as Chief Executive Tim Cook and others take a closer look at spending after several high-profile movie flops, including Argylle, the news outlet added, citing two people with direct knowledge of the matter.
  • Apple TV+ is a part of Apple's Services division, which hit a new all-time in revenue last quarter at $26.34B, up from $23.11B in the year-ago period.
  • Apple shares fell 0.5% in premarket trading.
  • Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: RLJ Lodging Trust announces retirement of CFO Sean Mahoney
data.description:
• RLJ Lodging Trust (NYSE:RLJ) on Thursday said following seven years at the company, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Sean Mahoney will be retiring in May.
  • To ensure a smooth transition, Mahoney will continue to serve as the company’s CFO until his departure in May and will actively support the transition plan.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Benson Hill files for chapter 11 bankruptcy
data.description:
• Benson Hill (NASDAQ:BHIL) said on Thursday that it and its subsidiaries filed voluntary petitions for relief under chapter 11 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code in the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware.
  • The company further disclosed that it intends to pursue a sale of its business, including a sale of all or a portion of the company’s assets, while continuing to support its farmers, partners, and customers during the Chapter 11 process.
  • To facilitate this process, in addition to having the use of its existing cash reserves, the company has received a commitment of approximately $11M in debtor-in-possession financing.
  • Following court approval, the company expects this financing to provide the necessary liquidity to support operations throughout the Chapter 11 process.
  • Benson Hill (BHIL) shares fell more than 33% in pre-market trading.
